summary: v2.0 Tier-1 production and helper evidence is present, but v2.0 is not release-ready.

checks.label: Browser Tier-1 helper foundation
checks.caveat: Helper-layer evidence only; not live production automation.

checks.label: Explainable Memory Ranking
checks.caveat: Deterministic local ranking evidence; not a full long-term memory system.

checks.label: Code Intel semantic fallback
checks.caveat: Heuristic lexical fallback; not embedding or LSP-grade semantics.

checks.label: Default registry exposure invariant
checks.caveat: Narrow default must remain coding plus Code Intel essentials.

checks.label: Production Browser automation proof
checks.caveat: Adapter-contract proof with transcript evidence; not default Browser exposure.

checks.label: Visual grounding proof
checks.caveat: Visual snapshot metadata proof; raw screenshot bytes are not stored in repository evidence.

checks.label: v2.0 Tier-2 blockers
checks.blocker: Tier-2 v2.0 blockers remain tracked separately
checks.caveat: Tier-2 blockers remain separate and unresolved.

blockers: Tier-2 v2.0 blockers remain tracked separately

defaultExposure.caveat: browser_action and browser_js are coding-surface helpers in the default registry, not production Browser completion proof.

nextActions: D130: close the next v2.0 Tier-2 blocker without expanding default exposure.
Keep Tier-2 v2.0 blockers separate from helper-layer evidence.
Keep Browser, Desktop, Channel, media, and productivity tools out of non-coding default exposure.
